name: Tasks View
description:
VS Code WebviewView sidebar panel showing the Code Workbench task board, plus the full editor-tab Task Board page, the Phase Board page, and the task-bound phase flow. Hosts the shared @code-workbench/ui TasksPanel React component in a webview and answers its list/create/update/remove/openInEditor/startPhase/confirmBulkStart RPC calls via the shared task-store. The RPC handler set and context payload are exported (buildTaskRpcHandlers, taskPanelContext) and shared with the full editor-tab Task Board page (tasksPage.ts, command codeWorkbench.tasks.openAsPage) and the Phase Board (phaseBoardPage.ts, command codeWorkbench.tasks.openPhaseBoard). The page pushes surface:'page' in its context, switching TasksPanel into pageMode: clicking a task opens an inline TaskDetailPane editor (title/description/memo/priority/status/worktree/epic/tags + subtasks; the backing .md file open is a secondary button), and a toolbar adds group-by (worktree/epic/flat), status filter (active/all/open/in-progress/done) and tag filter. Sidebar keeps the compact accordion behavior. The tasks file watcher (watchTasks) refreshes all three surfaces via refreshTaskSurfaces().

Task-bound phase flow (commands/taskFlow.ts): a root task carries an optional `phase` field ('plan'|'implement'|'review'|'fix'|null). CRITICAL SEMANTIC: `phase` names the phase to run NEXT, not the one already running — a Plan session hands off with phase:'implement', and startPhase re-writes the same value when it launches that phase. Both surfaces read it that way: TaskDetailPane's PhaseStepper marks steps before `phase` done, `phase` itself pending, and its single button starts PHASE_FLOW[pendingIdx]; the Phase Board puts a card in the column its `phase` names, and that column's Start button launches that same phase. (An earlier off-by-one had the stepper offering PHASE_FLOW[currentIdx+1], skipping a phase — do not reintroduce it.)

Phase prompts + models live in packages/mcp-core/phase-prompts.cjs — ONE source of truth for PHASE_ORDER, PHASE_META (label/icon/model/planOnly/effort), PHASE_DESCRIPTIONS, and the procedure text. Procedures carry a `{{TASK_ID}}` placeholder: phasePrompt(phase, task) swaps a real id for the session the board spawns; phasePromptBulk(phase, tasks) emits one prompt containing the same procedure once per task (falling back to phasePrompt for a single task); phaseSkillBody(phase) swaps `$ARGUMENTS` for the bundled skills. skills/cw-implement|cw-review|cw-fix.cjs are 3-line wrappers over phaseSkill(); cw-plan (the generic planning skill) covers the Plan phase, so no phase-generated cw-plan exists. The Plan procedure now tells the session to set `order` (and, for adjacent independent steps, matching `parallel: true`) on every plan-step subtask it files; the Implement procedure tells the session to work subtasks in `order` (unordered last, ties by creation time) and to dispatch same-order both-`parallel` subtasks as one concurrent subagent wave before moving to the next order.

Phase Board (phaseBoardPage.ts + ui PhaseBoard.tsx + webview/phaseboard.tsx): four columns — Plan | Implement | Review | Fix. A root task with no `phase` is inferred (plan-step subtasks ⇒ Implement, otherwise Plan). Subtasks and done-and-out-of-flow tasks never render as cards, but plan-step / review-finding subtask progress is summarized as chips on the parent. Each card's Start calls the shared startPhase RPC → codeWorkbench.tasks.startPhase.

Bulk phase start: each column has a footer "Start all <Phase> (N)" button (rendered only when N>0 and the host supplies confirmBulkStart). It calls the confirmBulkStart RPC → tasksView.confirmBulkStartPhase, a host-native `showWarningMessage(..., { modal: true }, ...)` — the codebase's only modal:true call. Buttons are `Start <N>` / `Include in-progress (<N+M>)` / Cancel; an undefined answer (Escape) is a Cancel. It then runs codeWorkbench.tasks.startPhaseBulk(ids, phase, includeInProgress), which de-dupes ids, re-reads each task's LIVE status (dropping ones deleted/done/in-progress since the board's snapshot), groups the survivors by the worktree each resolves to, and spawns ONE session per worktree group whose prompt (phasePromptBulk) tells it to run the phase for each task strictly one at a time, handing off each before reading the next. Bulk start is NOT a fan-out of one-session-per-task: N sessions in a shared worktree would interleave edits into a diff no Review phase can attribute. taskFlow.ts exports startTaskPhase (used by the single-card command) and startTaskPhaseBulk; a batch that fails to spawn marks every id in that group failed. TaskFlowError carries a `no-worktree` code so the single-card command can stay silent when the user backs out of the worktree picker. PhaseBoard keeps the partial-failure summary in a SEPARATE `bulkError` state, never the shared `error` state: the started sessions touch task files, watchTasks fires 'tasks-changed', reloadKey bumps, and the load effect's success branch calls setError(null) — which would wipe the summary within seconds. `bulkError` is cleared only by the next bulk click or an all-success batch.

Per-phase model overrides: GlobalPrefs.phaseModels + WorktreePrefs.phaseModels, resolved by SessionManager.resolvePhaseModel(worktree, phase) as worktree[phase] ?? global[phase] ?? PHASE_META[phase].model, where a 'default' value at either level means "keep falling through" (NOT "pass no --model flag", which is what 'default' means for the plain session model). normalizePhaseModels() in globalPrefs.ts drops unknown phases/models so a hand-edited settings.json can't smuggle a string into `claude --model`. Edited in the global settings panel (sec-phases) and the per-worktree Claude prefs panel (sec-phases, whose "inherit" option shows what it resolves to). phaseBoardPage pushes a resolved {fallback, byWorktreeKey} PhaseModelMap so each card shows the model its Start button will actually launch.

Supporting session-manager changes: SavedSession/CreateSessionOptions gained permissionMode (→ `claude --permission-mode`, mutually exclusive with yolo args) and effortOverride (takes precedence over the worktree's effort pref) so a Plan-phase session can force read-only planning and its own effort level.

TasksPanel's SubtaskRow now renders an `#N` chip when a subtask has a numeric `order` and a `∥` chip when `parallel` is set — the parallel flag existed on the data model for a while before this was surfaced in the UI. Subtask rows are sorted by a local `siblingCmp` (in buildChildMap) that must match task-format.cjs's exported `siblingCmp` exactly — the UI package is host-agnostic and can't `require` the CJS module, so the comparator is duplicated by hand rather than imported.
guidelines: All task file I/O goes through the shared task-store — never implement parallel task logic in the extension.
Watch task files with watchTasks() and call refreshTaskSurfaces() on change — it fans out to the sidebar, the Task Board page, and the Phase Board.
The panel UI is the shared ui TasksPanel / PhaseBoard — render it via a React webview, do not hand-write panel markup.
Task formatting/parsing belongs in mcp-core task-store, not the extension — tasks.ts is a thin re-export.
Page-only UI (detail editor, filter toolbar) is gated on the pageMode prop, driven by surface:'page' in the context event — the same React bundle serves both surfaces; never fork the bundle.

Phase prompt text and per-phase models live ONLY in mcp-core/phase-prompts.cjs. Adding a phase, changing a model default, or editing procedure wording happens there — the extension and the cw-* skills both read from it.
A task's `phase` is the phase to run NEXT. Any UI that renders or advances the flow must read it that way.
Phase model selects must exclude the 'default' ClaudeModel from their concrete options — they carry their own 'default' (= inherit) option.
`order` + `parallel` semantics (subtask sequencing/waves) are stated once in phase-prompts.cjs's Plan/Implement procedure text — do not restate or fork the rule elsewhere.
TasksPanel's child-sort comparator (siblingCmp in buildChildMap) must be kept byte-for-byte equivalent to task-format.cjs's exported siblingCmp when either changes.
Starting a phase for ONE task — a card, a palette command — goes through taskFlow.startTaskPhase. Starting a whole column goes through startTaskPhaseBulk, which batches per worktree into one session.

A bulk phase batch must instruct its session to work its tasks sequentially, each handed off before the next is read. Concurrency inside a phase belongs to the Implement procedure's subtask waves, not to the batch.
A bulk action that can spawn Claude sessions must be gated by a host-native modal confirmation whose button labels state the task count. The webview cannot be trusted to ask.
Ids sent from the board are a snapshot; any bulk host command must re-read live task status before acting on them.
PhaseBoard error state that must outlive a 'tasks-changed' reload (bulkError) belongs in its own state — the load effect clears `error` on every successful re-list.
anti_patterns: Do not implement task CRUD directly in the extension — delegate to the shared task-store.
Do not rebuild the task panel as vanilla-JS webview HTML — use the shared ui React component.
Do not reintroduce a local taskFormat module — formatting lives in the shared task-store.
Do not make opening the .md file the primary click action on the page — the inline TaskDetailPane is the editor; the file open is secondary.
Do not copy phase procedure text into taskFlow.ts or a skill file — generate it from phase-prompts.cjs, or the board and the skills will drift.
Do not treat `phase` as the currently-running phase (the old PhaseStepper off-by-one) — it names the NEXT phase, and the stepper would skip a step.
Do not read a phase's model straight from PHASE_META when spawning a session — go through SessionManager.resolvePhaseModel so global / per-worktree overrides apply.
Do not add a second `order`/`parallel` comparator that disagrees with task-format.cjs's siblingCmp — the board and the phase-flow session would then disagree on subtask sequencing.

Do not turn bulk start back into one session per task (its original shape) — sessions sharing a worktree interleave edits and produce an unattributable diff. One chat per worktree, tasks worked in sequence.
Do not batch tasks from different worktrees into one session — a phase edits the working tree it runs in.
Do not put a bulk partial-failure summary in PhaseBoard's shared `error` state — the tasks-changed reload wipes it seconds later.
Do not confirm a bulk start with a webview-side dialog, and do not skip the confirmation because the column looks small.
decisions: The tasks module is a thin re-export of the shared task-store.
The panel was migrated from a vanilla-JS webview script to a React webview rendering the shared ui TasksPanel; VS Code-level filter/show-done commands are kept as compatibility stubs.
Task formatting/parsing was consolidated into the shared task-store (mcp-core); the extension's taskFormat.ts was deleted to avoid divergence.
Page mode reuses the row-click onOpenTask hook internally for selection instead of adding a parallel selection API.
Phase procedures were extracted from taskFlow.ts into mcp-core/phase-prompts.cjs so the Start button and the hand-run /cw-<phase> skills share one source of truth.
The Phase Board is a separate editor-tab page rather than a tab inside the Task Board page — it needs full width, and it reuses buildTaskRpcHandlers rather than forking the tasks bundle.
Per-phase model overrides reuse the existing global-defaults + per-worktree-override merge pattern (getPrefs), rather than introducing a second settings mechanism.
PhaseBoard re-lists after startPhase resolves instead of waiting for the fs watcher, so the card moves columns immediately.

2026-07-10: surfaced the long-dormant `parallel` flag in TasksPanel's SubtaskRow (a `∩` chip) alongside a new `#N` order chip, rather than leaving both invisible in the UI while the phase flow started depending on them.

2026-07-10: bulk column start is host-native-confirmed (the codebase's only showWarningMessage modal:true) because a webview button must not be able to spawn sessions unchallenged, and it re-reads live status because board ids are a snapshot. Partial failures land in a dedicated PhaseBoard `bulkError` state so the tasks-changed reload the started sessions trigger cannot erase them. phaseBoardPage.ts needed no change — it inherits confirmBulkStart by spreading buildTaskRpcHandlers.

2026-07-10: bulk start was reshaped from one session per task (Promise.allSettled over startTaskPhase) into ONE session per worktree, driven by phasePromptBulk, which repeats the per-task procedure verbatim and orders the session to work them sequentially. N concurrent sessions in one working tree interleave their edits, so no later Review phase can attribute a hunk to a task; a single agent with a queue keeps each task's diff and handoff its own.
